page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.18xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance features an x86_64 architecture with 72 dedicated physical cores and 72 vCPUs, indicating a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 288.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for operating system and application data. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 33 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high vCPU density offer a predictable performance profile for enterprise workloads, avoiding the resource contention issues associated with shared-core or multi-threaded environments.

The balanced compute-to-memory ratio of 1:4 makes this instance suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications. Recommended workloads include medium-to-large databases, web serving fleets, containerized microservices, and continuous integration and delivery pipelines that benefit from high parallel processing capabilities and dedicated CPU resources without requiring specialized GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.18xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor on an x86_64 architecture, it delivers 72 dedicated vCPUs across 72 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 288.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps but does not include local storage or GPU hardware. This resource profile provides a predictable, non-shared compute environment. It is well-suited for enterprise workloads such as web serving, containerized applications, database hosting, and CI/CD pipelines that require high parallel processing capabilities without the need for specialized local storage or hardware accelerators.
